entries.title: Write one behavior per test
entries.content: Single-purpose tests fail with clearer intent and reduce time spent diagnosing what actually regressed.

entries.title: Rotate credentials on schedule
entries.content: Regular credential rotation limits blast radius if a secret leaks and encourages teams to maintain key management hygiene.

entries.title: Add indexes for real query patterns
entries.content: Measure slow queries first, then index based on predicates and sort order. Over-indexing harms write performance.

entries.title: Design for idempotency
entries.content: Idempotent create/update endpoints make retries safe under network failures and reduce accidental duplicate operations.

entries.title: Log with stable keys
entries.content: Structured logs with predictable field names make filtering and alerting far easier than free-form log lines.

entries.title: Optimize first contentful view
entries.content: Prioritizing critical rendering path assets improves perceived performance more than micro-optimizing non-critical code paths.

entries.title: Set realistic timeouts everywhere
entries.content: Explicit timeouts on outbound calls prevent thread exhaustion and keep cascading failures contained.

entries.title: Keep boundaries explicit
entries.content: Defining module boundaries early reduces accidental coupling and keeps refactors local instead of system-wide.

entries.title: Automate rollback paths
entries.content: A tested rollback mechanism shortens incident duration and increases confidence during high-frequency deployments.

entries.title: Name intent, not mechanics
entries.content: Readable names should communicate business intent so maintainers understand why code exists before how it works.

entries.title: Keep runbooks close to code
entries.content: Version-controlled operational runbooks age better than external docs and stay aligned with implementation changes.

entries.title: Batch similar tasks
entries.content: Grouping related cognitive tasks reduces context switching overhead and improves steady development throughput.

entries.title: Automate rollback paths
entries.content: A tested rollback mechanism shortens incident duration and increases confidence during high-frequency deployments.

entries.title: Name intent, not mechanics
entries.content: Readable names should communicate business intent so maintainers understand why code exists before how it works.

entries.title: Use exponential backoff with jitter
entries.content: Backoff plus jitter avoids retry storms and improves recovery behavior when downstream systems are degraded.

entries.title: Keep runbooks close to code
entries.content: Version-controlled operational runbooks age better than external docs and stay aligned with implementation changes.

entries.title: Batch similar tasks
entries.content: Grouping related cognitive tasks reduces context switching overhead and improves steady development throughput.
